calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Wendy Oconnor Lmft For Order Transferring Venue To Los Angeles County Delcaration Of Wendy Oconnor
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WENDY O'CONNOR, L.M.F.T.'s Motion For Order Transferring Venue To Los Angeles County is Off calendar per request of the moving party and approval of Judge Schulman during the hearing of 6/13/19.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Joinder In Defendant Brandy Cohen-Browns Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anthony Martinez, Esq. 949-261-2872 x1255 of Callahan Thompson Sherman & Caudill LLP for Defendant Wendy O'Connor through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WENDY O'CONNOR, L.M.F.T.'S Joinder In Defendant Brandy Cohen-Browns Motion To Change Venue.

Defendant Wendy O'Connor, L.M.F.T.'s joinder in Defendant Brandy Cohen-Brown, D.O.'s motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to be heard in Los Angeles Superior Court. Plaintiff shall pay the transfer fees.

"[T]he county where the defendants or some of them reside at the commencement of the action is the proper court for the trial of the action." (Code Civ. Proc. 395(a).) Both Defendants are residents of Los Angeles. Venue is only proper where the injury occurred when the injury is a physical injury to a person or personal property; the injury "does not include mere injury to reputation, business or personal feelings." (Plum v. Newhart (1931) 118 Cal.App. 73, 75.) Therefore, the relevant injury is not the injury to Plaintiff Pour Guys Soma LLC in being subjected to Jason Roussos' lawsuit. Rather, the relevant injury is the alleged injury to Mr. Roussos stemming from alleged misdiagnosis and mistreatment, which occurred in Los Angeles.

Furthermore, "nothing in the law provides for venue to be determined at the commencement of the action based on the convenience of plaintiff's witnesses." (Cholakian & Assocs. v. Superior Court (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 361, 373.) Plaintiff has not met its burden to show that a transfer of venue on that ground is warranted. In any event, Plaintiff does not need thirty-seven firefighters, doctors, police officers, and other witnesses to the underlying assault to determine whether Defendants violated their standard of care in treating Mr. Roussos. Plaintiff can arrange to have any key witnesses, such as its one expert witness, transported to Los Angeles.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) In addition, the Court takes the Motion by Wendy O'Connor for Order transferring Venue to Los Angeles scheduled for June 20, 2019 off-calendar.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anthony Martinez, Esq. 949-261-2872 x1255 of Callahan Thompson Sherman & Caudill LLP for Defendant Wendy O'Connor through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRANDY COHEN-BROWN, D.O.'S Motion To Change Venue.

Defendant Brandy Cohen-Brown, D.O.'s motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to be heard in Los Angeles Superior Court. Plaintiff shall pay the transfer fees.

"[T]he county where the defendants or some of them reside at the commencement of the action is the proper court for the trial of the action." (Code Civ. Proc. 395(a).) Both Defendants are residents of Los Angeles. Venue is only proper where the injury occurred when the injury is a physical injury to a person or personal property; the injury "does not include mere injury to reputation, business or personal feelings." (Plum v. Newhart (1931) 118 Cal.App. 73, 75.) Therefore, the relevant injury is not the injury to Plaintiff Pour Guys Soma LLC in being subjected to Jason Roussos' lawsuit. Rather, the relevant injury is the alleged injury to Mr. Roussos stemming from alleged misdiagnosis and mistreatment, which occurred in Los Angeles.

Furthermore, "nothing in the law provides for venue to be determined at the commencement of the action based on the convenience of plaintiff's witnesses." (Cholakian & Assocs. v. Superior Court (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 361, 373.) Plaintiff has not met its burden to show that a transfer of venue on that ground is warranted. In any event, Plaintiff does not need thirty-seven firefighters, doctors, police officers, and other witnesses to the underlying assault to determine whether Defendants violated their standard of care in treating Mr. Roussos. Plaintiff can arrange to have any key witnesses, such as its one expert witness, transported to Los Angeles.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) In addition, the Court takes the Motion by Wendy O'Connor for Order transferring Venue to Los Angeles scheduled for June 20, 2019 off-calendar.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
